methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $144M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Atomic Machines is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jeff Holden and Prashant Patil that has raised $144M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-tech peers in chips and related domains, including Substrate, Etched, Cerebras, and PsiQuantum at comparable or adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $144M total raised, implying $288M. Peer-median anchoring across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Substrate at $1.0B Series A, Etched at $5.0B Series B, Positron AI at $1.0B Series B) yields a median of $1.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied absent revenue disclosure, typical of private companies. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods across their low, median, and high points produces a valuation range of $245M to $1.1B, with a median of $644M. The wide range reflects tension between stage-based progression methods, which anchor closer to capital deployed, and peer-median approaches weighted toward later-stage and higher-valued comparables. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given the nascency of frontier-chip markets, and the absence of revenue visibility required for public-market benchmarking.
